questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: In the context of the Indian Cybercrime Coordination Centre (I4C) and the e-Zero FIR initiative, which of the following describes the correct functional workflow for a cyber financial crime complaint?

options.text: Complaint on 1930 helpline -> Automatic regular FIR at territorial station -> Investigation by I4C.
Complaint on NCRP -> Automatic Zero FIR at e-Crime Police Station -> Routing to territorial Cyber Crime Police Station.
Complaint at local station -> Manual entry into NCRP -> Transfer to I4C for Zero FIR registration.
Complaint on 1930 helpline -> Verification by Delhi High Court -> Registration of e-Zero FIR at I4C headquarters.

questions.explanation: The workflow involves reporting via the National Cybercrime Reporting Portal (NCRP) or the 1930 helpline. If the loss is above Rs. 10 lakh, it automatically leads to the registration of a Zero FIR with the e-Crime Police Station of Delhi, which is then immediately routed to the territorial Cyber Crime Police Stations.
questions.takeaway: The e-Zero FIR mechanism automates the initial registration and jurisdictional routing for high-value cyber financial crimes reported through centralized digital channels.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Financial losses above threshold limit of Rs. 10 lakh made to National Cybercrime Reporting Portal (NCRP) or 1930 helpline will automatically lead to registration of a Zero FIR with e-Crime Police Station of Delhi. This will be immediately routed to the territorial Cyber Crime Police Stations.
